description_long: This object is a compact, rectangular electronic device with a beige plastic casing and a metallic front panel featuring Russian text and several buttons. The front panel includes a display window at the top, likely for digital readouts, and a set of labeled buttons and switches for operational control. The labels and markings suggest it is a radiometer or dosimeter, specifically the 'Pripyat' model, used for measuring radiation levels. The back side has a battery compartment cover and additional labeling, including a serial number and technical information. The sides are plain and unadorned, emphasizing the utilitarian design. The overall construction appears robust, suitable for handheld or portable use in urban or industrial environments.
description: A beige, rectangular radiation measurement device with a metallic control panel, display window, and labeled buttons, designed for portable use.
description_view_0: The front view shows the metallic control panel with Russian labels, a display window, and several operational buttons.
description_view_1: The left side view is plain, showing the thickness of the device without any visible features.
description_view_2: The back view reveals a battery compartment cover, technical labeling, and a serial number.
description_view_3: The right side view is also plain, mirroring the left side with no distinguishing features.
